iteration_posture: ITERATE_NOT_REWRITE — all four blockers are repairable through targeted plan edits; no structural rework required (consistent with project memory project_phase2_plan_review_verdict.md)
summary: Rev-3 added Phase 4A (Task 0) to retire the Phase 3 module-tail monkeypatching before frontend work. The cleanup direction is correct, but rev-3 introduces four new BLOCKING issues spread across architecture, quality, and systems: (1) F-01 — Task 0 Step 2 wording is ambiguous and may migrate a forbidden defensive try/except into the canonical constructor body; (2) TS-1 — Task 0 Step 5 verification list is too narrow (6 files) given 20+ test files touch the constructor surface, and Task 9 has no backend re-run; (3) B-1 — new sessionStore fields (recoveryError, recoveryStartedEditorRevision) are not added to the 4 explicit session-switch reset sites, mirroring the exact pre-clearedGuidedState() drift pattern; (4) B-2 — editorRevision is introduced but its increment trigger is unspecified, leaving the concurrent-edit guard structurally unable to do its job. F-01 violates CLAUDE.md Defensive Programming primacy. B-1 and B-2 are systems-correctness issues. TS-1 is a verification-completeness issue. All can be addressed with focused plan edits for a rev-4.

conflicts_resolved.issue: Task 9 final gate omits backend re-run
conflicts_resolved.resolution: Consolidated as single finding W-FINAL-GATE. Both reviewers identify the same gap: Task 9 runs only npm tests, so the Phase 4A backend cleanup is not re-verified at PR-open time after Tasks 1-8 may have iterated. Architecture's required_fix and quality's recommendation are the same — add a backend pytest invocation as Task 9 Step 0. Treated as one warning, not two.
conflicts_resolved.outcome: Single warning; cross-references both source IDs

conflicts_resolved.issue: editorRevision vs compositionState.version (dual source of truth)
conflicts_resolved.relationship: Related but distinct. W-01 asks: 'why a second counter when compositionState.version already exists?' B-2 asks: 'if you keep editorRevision, what increments it?' Both dissolve under the same fix.
conflicts_resolved.resolution: Synthesized fix: snapshot compositionState.version at compose-start (captured as recoveryStartedCompositionVersion), compare against current compositionState?.version at Apply time. This eliminates the parallel counter (resolves W-01), eliminates the undefined-increment-trigger question (resolves B-2 by deferring increment discipline to whichever component already mutates compositionState), and uses the field that already exists. B-2 retains BLOCKING severity because the guard is structurally non-functional without resolution; W-01 demoted to context for the fix.
conflicts_resolved.outcome: B-2 retained as BLOCKING with the W-01 fix folded into the resolution

conflicts_resolved.issue: Reality W-NEW-1 (uv run pytest vs .venv/bin/python -m pytest) and Quality TS-1 recommendation
conflicts_resolved.potential_conflict: Quality TS-1's recommendation literally says 'PYTHONPATH=src uv run pytest -q tests/unit/web/composer/ tests/property/web/composer/' — reusing the form reality flagged as a convention violation.
conflicts_resolved.resolution: Reality is correct on convention (CLAUDE.md prescribes .venv/bin/python -m pytest; the entire Phase 3 plan uses it 25 times). Quality's substantive point (expand scope) stands; only the invocation form should change. Recommended consolidated form: .venv/bin/python -m pytest -q tests/unit/web/composer/ tests/property/web/composer/
conflicts_resolved.outcome: W-NEW-1 remains as warning; TS-1 resolution updated to use the conventional invocation form

conflicts_resolved.issue: Systems pr_scope (Task 0 split-as-prerequisite PR) vs Architecture W-04 (one PR per phase policy)
conflicts_resolved.potential_conflict: Systems recommends splitting Task 0 to reduce reviewer cognitive load; Architecture notes the one-PR-per-phase policy is the operator's deliberate choice.
conflicts_resolved.resolution: Both reviewers flag this as non-blocking. Architecture defers to the operator's policy; systems agrees the split is optional. Synthesized as a single INFO-level recommendation: be prepared to split if Phase 4B is delayed, but do not split prophylactically.

blocking_issues.title: Plan wording may preserve forbidden defensive try/except in constructor
blocking_issues.issue: Current _phase3_composer_init uses `try: self._max_tool_calls_per_turn = self._settings.composer_max_tool_calls_per_turn / except AttributeError: 16`. WebSettings.composer_max_tool_calls_per_turn is unconditionally defined (Pydantic Field default=16, ge=1), so the field cannot raise AttributeError on a correctly-constructed settings object. Task 0 Step 2 reads '_max_tool_calls_per_turn, using self._settings.composer_max_tool_calls_per_turn with the existing fallback value 16' — 'with the existing fallback value 16' is ambiguous and could be interpreted as preserving the try/except pattern in the cleaned-up constructor. CLAUDE.md §'Defensive Programming: Forbidden' bans this pattern.
blocking_issues.required_fix: Rewrite Task 0 Step 2 to: '_max_tool_calls_per_turn, assigned directly from self._settings.composer_max_tool_calls_per_turn without try/except or runtime fallback — the Pydantic model guarantees the field is always present (default=16). Test fixtures must construct settings with the field set.' Add a brief note explaining why the fallback is being deleted, not migrated.

blocking_issues.title: Verification scope after constructor refactor is too narrow; final gate has no backend re-run
blocking_issues.issue: Task 0 Step 5 runs 6 test files. Moving 8 instance attributes into the real constructor would silently break any test that instantiates ComposerServiceImpl() and reaches those code paths. grep finds ~20 test files reading _phase3_last_* / _max_tool_calls_per_turn / _telemetry / _redaction_telemetry and 10 files instantiating ComposerServiceImpl() directly — far beyond the Step 5 allow-list. tests/integration/pipeline/test_composer_llm_eval_characterization.py:262 also calls _run_one_turn_for_test but is not in Step 5. Task 9 runs only npm test, providing no backend regression signal at PR-open time. Consolidates with architecture W-02 (same gap).
evidence: Quality report grep evidence: 20 files read phase3 instance attributes; 10 files instantiate ComposerServiceImpl()
Plan Task 9 Step 1: 'cd src/elspeth/web/frontend && npm test' — no Python step
blocking_issues.required_fix: Replace Task 0 Step 5 invocation with: `.venv/bin/python -m pytest -q tests/unit/web/composer/ tests/property/web/composer/ tests/integration/pipeline/test_composer_llm_eval_characterization.py` (uses CLAUDE.md-compliant form per W-NEW-1; covers all 10 constructor-instantiating files plus the integration eval test). Add Task 9 Step 0 (before npm test) re-running the same invocation to gate the final PR. This single fix closes B-TS1, architecture W-02, quality TS-8, and the integration eval coverage gap.

blocking_issues.title: Cross-session state leak — recoveryError / recoveryStartedEditorRevision not added to 4 session-switch reset sites
blocking_issues.issue: sessionStore has 4 explicit session-context reset sites (sessionStore.ts:175 createSession, :211 archiveSession active-session branch, :237 selectSession, :547 forkFromMessage). guidedMode previously hit this exact drift problem; the team extracted clearedGuidedState() at lines 89-99 to make new field additions structurally enforced. Task 3 Step 2 adds new fields (recoveryError, recoveryStartedEditorRevision — or recoveryStartedCompositionVersion after B-SYS-B2 fix) without listing the reset sites. A user opening the panel on session A, switching to session B without dismissing, then applying recovery, would install session A's composition state into session B.
evidence: sessionStore.ts:89-99 — clearedGuidedState() helper, comment explaining its drift-prevention purpose
sessionStore.ts:175, 211, 237, 547 — four reset sites currently using clearedGuidedState() but not the new recovery fields
blocking_issues.required_fix: Task 3 Step 2 must (a) introduce clearedRecoveryState() helper analogous to clearedGuidedState() and (b) explicitly list all 4 session-switch reset sites (createSession, archiveSession active-session branch, selectSession, forkFromMessage) with the helper composed in. Task 3 Step 1 should add a test asserting that selectSession clears recovery panel state. This is the same discipline guidedMode already enforces — replicate it.

blocking_issues.title: editorRevision increment trigger undefined — concurrent-edit guard is structurally non-functional
blocking_issues.issue: Plan introduces editorRevision but does not specify what increments it. If editorRevision only advances on server-confirmed compositionState updates, the guard misses local edits made between compose-start and Apply (the exact case it is supposed to catch). Architecture W-01 independently observes that compositionState.version already exists and serves this purpose. Synthesized fix dissolves both findings: capture compositionState.version at compose-start, compare against compositionState?.version at Apply.
evidence: Systems grep: no match for editorRevision, editCounter, edit_counter, compositionRevision anywhere in src/elspeth/web/frontend/src/
Architecture: src/elspeth/web/frontend/src/types/index.ts:159 — CompositionState.version exists; sessionStore.ts:305-308 already uses it for change detection
Plan Task 3 Step 2 — 'editorRevision: number or equivalent current edit counter' with no increment-trigger specification
blocking_issues.required_fix: Replace editorRevision with recoveryStartedCompositionVersion: number | null. Set it at compose-start (when sendMessage/retryMessage is invoked) to the current compositionState?.version. At Apply time, compare against the current compositionState?.version — if changed, show the concurrent-edit confirmation. This (a) eliminates the dual-source-of-truth (W-01), (b) defers increment discipline to whichever component already mutates compositionState (which is server-confirmed updates, the correct semantics for 'did the canonical state change?'), (c) makes the guard mechanically verifiable. If the team genuinely needs to catch in-editor local edits before server confirmation, that requires a separate field with explicit increment paths enumerated in Task 3 Step 2 — but the spec §7.2 requirement is 'state was edited' which compositionState.version covers.

warnings.issue: Task 0 Step 5 uses `PYTHONPATH=src uv run pytest -q ...` while CLAUDE.md and the Phase 3 plan series consistently use `.venv/bin/python -m pytest`. uv run resolves its own interpreter and may pick up a different Python version than the activated venv (project memory: tier-model FPs are Python-version sensitive).
warnings.resolution: Change to `.venv/bin/python -m pytest -q ...`. Folds into B-TS1 fix.

warnings.issue: Final phase gate runs only npm test. After 7 tasks of iteration, no backend signal confirms Phase 4A cleanup still holds.
warnings.resolution: Add Task 9 Step 0 (before npm test): `.venv/bin/python -m pytest -q tests/unit/web/composer/ tests/property/web/composer/ tests/integration/pipeline/test_composer_llm_eval_characterization.py`. Same invocation as B-TS1 fix.

warnings.issue: AST guard does not cover _PHASE3_ORIGINAL_COMPOSER_INIT module-level Name assignment (service.py:4225). If only line 4247 is removed but line 4225 is left, the guard passes with dead code.
warnings.resolution: Extend the AST guard with a second loop covering ast.Name targets matching _PHASE3_* pattern. Also update the rg-check in Step 6 to include `_PHASE3_ORIGINAL_COMPOSER_INIT`.

warnings.issue: Test does not assert nested failed_turn sub-fields (assistant_message_id, tool_calls_attempted, tool_responses_persisted, transcript_url). A parseResponse bug truncating the failed_turn object would not be caught.
warnings.resolution: Add assertions in Task 1 Step 1 test that parsed ApiError.failed_turn contains all four sub-fields including transcript_url === null for the documented null case.

warnings.issue: Test does not assert fetchRecoveryTranscript URL contains include_tool_rows=true. Backend audit emission is gated on this parameter; a refactor that drops it would silently break audit-of-record without test failure.
warnings.resolution: Add URL-content assertion in the RecoveryTranscript test (regex /messages\?.*include_tool_rows=true/ or URLSearchParams inspection). Pairs naturally with W-SOE-3 fetch-once test.

warnings.issue: No test asserts redaction markers are visible in rendered transcript and raw sensitive content is NOT. A component that bypasses redaction would not be caught.
warnings.resolution: Add two test cases to RecoveryTranscript.test.tsx: (1) row with `<redacted-argument: summarized>` renders the marker; (2) row whose raw_content has non-redacted value does NOT render it when include_raw_content is false.

warnings.issue: discardRecovery test does not require asserting recoveryError === null after discard. A leftover error state could re-open the panel on the next render.
warnings.resolution: Add to Task 3 Step 1 test list: 'after discardRecovery(), store.recoveryError === null and recoveryStartedCompositionVersion === null'.

warnings.issue: Focus restoration only tested for the single close path. Three other close paths (Apply with no conflict, Apply→confirm, Apply→cancel-confirmation) are not covered.
warnings.resolution: Expand Task 6 Step 1 test list to cover all four close paths with explicit focus-restoration assertions.

warnings.issue: RecoveryTranscript useEffect not gated; could fetch on every parent re-render, incrementing composer.audit.audit_grade_view_total without bound. Spec §8.3.2 counter has no SLO cap.
warnings.resolution: Task 5 Step 2 must specify useEffect dependency array [sessionId, failedTurnAssistantMessageId]. Task 5 Step 1 must assert fetchRecoveryTranscript is called exactly once per panel open, not per render. Folds naturally with W-TS4 URL-content assertion.

warnings.issue: Retry-after-failure opens a second panel; plan does not specify whether the second open replaces or merges with the first.
warnings.resolution: Task 3 Step 2 makes 'replace' semantics explicit. Task 3 Step 1 adds test case: retry-after-discard surfaces newer error object; discardRecovery is idempotent.

warnings.issue: Recovery panel z-index unspecified; could collide with fanout-guard ConfirmDialog at --z-dialog (201) or render below at --z-overlay (100).
warnings.resolution: Task 6 Step 2 explicitly assigns --z-dialog (201). Task 8 Step 2 smoke includes simultaneous fanout-guard + recovery-panel visibility check.

warnings.issue: applyRecoveredState server-sync contract undefined — purely local UI mutation or triggers server-side state promotion?
warnings.resolution: Task 3 Step 2 explicitly states 'Apply is local-only; the partial_state remains on the server as the durable audit record; compositionState reflects the applied partial_state until the next user compose action persists it.' Task 3 Step 1 asserts no PATCH/PUT fires during applyRecoveredState.

warnings.issue: No schema-version guard on partial_state deserialization; backend deploy between persist and panel-open could cause silent shape mismatch.
warnings.resolution: Task 1 Step 2 documents this as accepted gap (low likelihood: single-session window). No code change required for Phase 4; flagged for future hardening.

warnings.issue: limit=500 may not contain the relevant failed-turn assistant row for sessions with >500 combined assistant+tool rows. Degraded-path test only covers null assistant_message_id.
warnings.resolution: Task 5 Step 1 adds degraded-path test: assistant_message_id present but not found in fetch window → degraded diagnostic identical to the null case.

warnings.issue: Phase 3 structural scruffiness flagged in rev-2 W3.
warnings.resolution: RESOLVED — Task 0 makes the cleanup a Phase 4A prerequisite with AST guard and focused behaviour tests. Carried for traceability.

warnings.issue: Spec/baseline drift around Modal pattern and transcript_url shape.
warnings.resolution: RESOLVED — Plan carries explicit current-code baseline notes.

warnings.issue: Recovery validation remains VER-only until a real failed compose flow is exercised end to end.
warnings.resolution: DEFERRED by design — VAL ownership stays with elspeth-90b4542b63 (release:rc5). Plan's Phase 4 Done When item 10 acknowledges this. No Phase 4 action required.

warnings.issue: Doc count fix (38 → 39 in Phase 3 plan appendix) piggybacked into structural refactor commit. Reviewer skimming `refactor(composer):` commit may not notice doc amendment.
warnings.resolution: Split Task 0 Step 4 into a standalone doc-only commit before the service.py refactor commit (`docs(plan):` prefix; --no-verify per project convention for markdown-only commits).

warnings.issue: Phase 4A and Phase 4B share a PR despite different risk profiles.
warnings.resolution: No code change. PR description should document that Phase 4A is independently mergeable if Phase 4B is delayed. Non-blocking — operator's policy choice.

informational.note: Null handling for assistant_message_id and transcript_url is correct Tier 3 trust-boundary behaviour, not defensive programming.
fetchRecoveryTranscript limit:500 with no cursor is a deliberate documented constraint matching the current backend API contract.
Phase 4A backend changes remain within L3; no cross-layer concerns.
Accessibility audit is manual-only (axe/Lighthouse). Consider adding jest-axe/vitest-axe automated gate in RecoveryPanel.test.tsx to prevent silent a11y regression. Non-blocking; useful enhancement.

Apply/Discard user actions emit no structured event. Plan should explicitly state this is intentional (the composition_states row persisted during the failed compose IS the audit record; Apply only changes in-memory state).
Audit-grade access round-trip from frontend trigger remains manually verified only. Task 7 integration test could assert audit_access_log row presence using existing TestClient + SQLite conftest.
Plan should explicitly state recovery panel is an always-on, no-feature-flag feature gated solely on response shape.
Prior review W2 (VER-only) remains open by design (VAL owner elspeth-90b4542b63). Confirm ticket carries release:rc5 label before merge.
Add 'null source in partial_state (valid pipeline)' to Task 4 Step 1 test list explicitly.
Add note to Task 5 Step 2 that fetchRecoveryTranscript must not pass include_raw_content and component must not render raw_content even if populated by a future API change.

next_revision_checklist.edit: Task 0 Step 2: Rewrite to 'assigned directly from self._settings.composer_max_tool_calls_per_turn without try/except or runtime fallback — the Pydantic model guarantees the field is always present (default=16)'. Closes B-F01.

Task 0 Step 5 + new Task 9 Step 0: Use `.venv/bin/python -m pytest -q tests/unit/web/composer/ tests/property/web/composer/ tests/integration/pipeline/test_composer_llm_eval_characterization.py`. Closes B-TS1, W-NEW-1, W-FINAL-GATE, W-TS8.
Task 0 Step 1 AST guard: extend to detect module-level ast.Name targets matching _PHASE3_* (catches _PHASE3_ORIGINAL_COMPOSER_INIT). Update Step 6 rg-check similarly. Closes W-TS2.
Task 0 Step 4: Move appendix count fix (38→39) to a standalone doc-only commit before Step 7 refactor commit (use `docs(plan):` prefix; --no-verify per project convention). Closes W-ARCH-W03.

Task 3 Step 2: Replace editorRevision with recoveryStartedCompositionVersion: number | null; set at compose-start to current compositionState?.version; compare at Apply. Closes B-SYS-B2 and W-01 (dual source of truth).

Task 3 Step 2: Introduce clearedRecoveryState() helper analogous to clearedGuidedState(); explicitly list and update all 4 session-switch reset sites (createSession :175, archiveSession :211, selectSession :237, forkFromMessage :547). Closes B-SYS-B1.

Task 3 Step 1: Add tests — (a) discardRecovery clears recoveryError and recoveryStartedCompositionVersion to null; (b) selectSession clears recovery state; (c) retry-after-discard replaces recoveryError with newer error; (d) discardRecovery is idempotent. Closes W-TS6, W-SOE-4.
Task 3 Step 2: Document applyRecoveredState as local-only (no PATCH/PUT); the partial_state remains the server-side audit record. Closes W-SOE-6.
Task 1 Step 1: Add assertions for all four nested failed_turn sub-fields (assistant_message_id, tool_calls_attempted, tool_responses_persisted, transcript_url including null case). Closes W-TS3.

Task 5 Step 1 + Step 2: (a) Assert fetchRecoveryTranscript URL contains include_tool_rows=true; (b) specify useEffect dependency array [sessionId, failedTurnAssistantMessageId] for fetch-once semantics; (c) assert single fetch per panel open; (d) add redaction-rendering positive + negative tests; (e) add degraded-path test for assistant_message_id beyond limit=500 window; (f) note fetchRecoveryTranscript must not pass include_raw_content. Closes W-TS4, W-TS5, W-SOE-3, W-PAGINATION, I-EC-2.
Task 6 Step 1: Expand focus-restoration tests to all four close paths (Discard, Apply no-conflict, Apply→confirm, Apply→cancel-confirmation). Closes W-TS7.
Task 6 Step 2: Explicitly assign --z-dialog (201) to recovery panel backdrop and panel. Task 8 Step 2 smoke checklist includes simultaneous fanout-guard + recovery-panel scenario. Closes W-SOE-5.
Task 1 Step 2: Document partial_state schema-version absence as accepted gap (no code change). Closes W-SOE-7.

Task 7 Step 2 or Phase 4 Done When: State explicitly that recovery panel is always-on (no feature flag) gated solely on response shape. Add note to Task 7 implementation that Apply/Discard are intentionally non-audited at user-action layer (composition_states row IS the audit-of-record). Closes I-PR-1, I-OBS-1.
Task 4 Step 1: Add 'null source in partial_state' to explicit test list. Closes I-EC-1.

verdict_rationale: CHANGES_REQUESTED is mandatory because four reviewers report blocking issues totaling four distinct findings (F-01, TS-1, B-1, B-2) spanning the defensive-programming policy boundary, verification completeness, cross-session state isolation, and concurrent-edit guard correctness. None require structural rework: all four are addressable through focused plan edits within Task 0 and Task 3. The plan's overall shape (Phase 4A cleanup + Phase 4B frontend feature) is sound; rev-2 was already approved-with-warnings, and rev-3's new Task 0 addresses the prior structural concern (W3) — the regressions are at the seams of the new task. Recommend iteration to rev-4 rather than rewrite, consistent with project memory project_phase2_plan_review_verdict.md.
